summary: Review of external-review improvements: InjectOsloPolicyConfig changed from in-place mutation to pure function returning new map (consistent with all other config/ functions), RenderPastePipeline now returns error on unrecognised Position instead of silently skipping (fail-fast), ValidatePolicyRules defaults fldPath to field.NewPath('rules') when nil. All changes are well-tested with mutation guards and edge-case coverage. Tests pass, coverage 99.3%, go vet clean, golangci-lint clean, gosec clean.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases are covered (nil, empty, boundary)
Error cases tested with specific assertions
Mutation guard tests verify input immutability

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (pure functions, sorted output)
No dead code or unused variables
Consistent error message format across packages

security_checklist.item: Pure functions with no I/O or Kubernetes API calls

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
InjectOsloPolicyConfig now consistent with all other config/ functions (returns new map)
Fail-fast on invalid Position is correct design (vs. silent skip)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Nil fldPath handled defensively with sensible default

issues_found.description: Docstring says 'Returns a new map without mutating the input' but when policyFilePath is empty (line 109), the function returns the original config reference, not a new map. This is a valid optimization (no-op case), but the doc is technically inaccurate for that path.
issues_found.fix: Consider updating the docstring to: 'When policyFilePath is non-empty, returns a new map without mutating the input. When empty, returns the input config unchanged.' — or accept as-is since callers should treat returned maps as read-only in the pipeline.

suggested_improvements: Minor: InjectOsloPolicyConfig docstring could clarify that the empty-path case returns the original reference (not a copy) — current wording 'Returns a new map' is inaccurate for that code path. Low priority since callers use this in a read-only pipeline.
